title: Projectiles from a Height
description: Extends the introductory projectile motion of kinematics-suvat to launches from an elevated point, derives the general range and time-of-flight results, finds the angle that maximises range, and tackles harder maximum-height and landing-on-a-slope style problems.
objectives: Extend the independent horizontal/vertical component model to projectiles launched from a height above the landing point
Derive and apply the time-of-flight and range formulas for a projectile launched from height h
Prove that, for a launch from ground level, range is maximised at a launch angle of 45 degrees
Solve maximum-height and landing-speed problems for elevated launches
Interpret a graph of range against launch angle, including how the elevated-launch case departs from the level-ground 45 degree symmetry
